questions.q: Who wrote the Harry Potter series?
questions.fact: J.K. Rowling wrote the seven-book series between 1997 and 2007. Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone was rejected by 12 publishers before being accepted!

questions.q: What is the name of the dragon guarding the treasure in The Hobbit?
questions.fact: Smaug is one of literature's most famous dragons. He has lived under the Lonely Mountain for 171 years by the time Bilbo arrives.

questions.q: In which novel does the character Atticus Finch appear?
questions.fact: Harper Lee's Pulitzer Prize-winning novel was published in 1960 and has sold over 45 million copies worldwide.

questions.q: Who wrote Pride and Prejudice?
questions.fact: Jane Austen published Pride and Prejudice in 1813. It opens with one of literature's most famous lines about a man in possession of a fortune.

questions.q: What is the first book in C.S. Lewis's Chronicles of Narnia series?
options: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
questions.fact: Although The Magician's Nephew comes first chronologically, The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe was published first in 1950 and is considered the series opener.

questions.q: Who wrote Charlie and the Chocolate Factory?
questions.fact: Roald Dahl published Charlie and the Chocolate Factory in 1964. The character of Willy Wonka was partly inspired by his own love of sweets as a child.

questions.q: In which novel does a girl follow a white rabbit down a hole?
questions.fact: Lewis Carroll published Alice's Adventures in Wonderland in 1865. He originally told the story to Alice Liddell, the daughter of a colleague, during a rowing trip.

questions.q: What is the name of Sherlock Holmes's address?
questions.fact: 221B Baker Street is one of the most famous fictional addresses in the world. The Sherlock Holmes Museum is now located there and receives thousands of letters addressed to Holmes every year.

questions.q: Who wrote The Secret Garden?
questions.fact: Frances Hodgson Burnett published The Secret Garden in 1911. She also wrote Little Lord Fauntleroy and A Little Princess.

questions.q: Which Shakespeare play features the line 'To be or not to be, that is the question'?
questions.fact: This is the opening of Hamlet's most famous soliloquy in Act 3, Scene 1. Hamlet contemplates whether it is better to endure suffering or take action against it.

questions.q: Who is the author of The Hunger Games trilogy?
questions.fact: Suzanne Collins was inspired to write The Hunger Games while channel-surfing between reality TV and news coverage of the Iraq War.

questions.q: What school does Matilda attend in Roald Dahl's Matilda?
questions.fact: Crunchem Hall is run by the terrifying Miss Trunchbull. The kindly Miss Honey provides the contrast that helps Matilda flourish.

questions.q: Which dystopian novel features a society controlled by 'the Party' and its leader 'Big Brother'?
questions.fact: George Orwell published Nineteen Eighty-Four in 1949. The phrase 'Big Brother is watching you' has entered everyday language as a warning about surveillance.

questions.q: In Anne of Green Gables, where does Anne come to live?
questions.fact: L.M. Montgomery set her beloved novel on Prince Edward Island in Canada, where she grew up. The farmhouse 'Green Gables' is a real heritage site you can visit today.

questions.q: What is the subtitle of Mary Shelley's Frankenstein?
questions.fact: Mary Shelley wrote Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus in 1818 when she was just 18 years old, after a ghost story competition with Percy Shelley and Lord Byron.

questions.q: When Harry arrives at the station near Platform 9 and Three Quarters, which family does he encounter?
questions.fact: During filming, Daniel Radcliffe and Rupert Grint kept messing up in takes so they could keep eating the sweets on the train.

questions.q: Harry is locked up in house where...?
questions.fact: The first letter from Hogwarts to Harry was addressed to him specifically at: "Mr H. Potter, The Cupboard under the Stairs, 4 Privet Drive, Little Whinging, Surrey". This showed the Dursleys that the magical world knew exactly how they were treating him.

questions.q: When Harry and Ron first meet Hermione, what is the very first thing she says?
options: Has anyone seen a toad? Neville's lost one.
questions.fact: In both the book and movie, she points out that Ron has dirt on his nose, which is a subtle detail that sets up her attentive, sometimes pedantic nature.

questions.q: Which famous trio were nearly not friends?
questions.fact: Because Hermione came across as so arrogant and bossy, Harry and Ron actually disliked her immediately and didn't become true friends with her until after they saved her from the troll on Halloween.

questions.q: Who announces that the Dursley family are moving?
questions.fact: Vernon Dursley states, 'That's it, we're moving!' to his wife and Dudley as he cannot stand Harry's letters anymore.

questions.q: What book did Hermione say she read when she first enters the Great Hall?
questions.fact: Despite her reputation for infallible memory, Hermione frequently forgot the specific details of what she read or couldn't apply them to new, magical situations, highlighting her humanity rather than just her intelligence.

questions.q: Who pretends to faint during the Halloween feast?
questions.fact: Quirrell's faint was a calculated diversion, not a sign of fear. As he explains later, he has a special gift with trolls, making his performance of being terrified by one particularly deceptive. He had already personally lured the troll into the castle himself.

questions.q: What are Hermione, Ron and Harry often referred to as?
questions.fact: The phrase was coined by the Harry Potter fandom to represent the trio's friendship as the "gold standard" of the series. Rowling actually used the term "HRH" (Harry, Ron, Hermione) for short in her personal writing, while screenwriter Steve Kloves simply used "the trio" in the film scripts.

questions.q: Who do the Golden Trio suspect is planning on taking the Sorcerer's Stone?
questions.fact: When Snape's leg was mauled, the trio assumed it was from trying to get past the three-headed dog, Fluffy. In reality, he was bitten while trying to stop Quirrell, who was acting on Voldemort's orders.

questions.q: What chapter of a book did Hermione find information about the Sorcerer's Stone?
questions.fact: Hermione actually possessed the answer weeks before, having checked out Magick Moste Evile or a similar heavy alchemy tome for "light reading".

questions.q: What names do Ron call Hermione throughout the whole book?
questions.fact: His most famous line about her in this book, spoken to Harry after she corrects his pronunciation of Wingardium Leviosa, is: "She's a nightmare, honestly. No wonder she hasn't got any friends".

questions.q: When Harry is in Hospital Wing, what sweets is he eating?
questions.fact: Dumbledore tells Harry that when he was young, he had the misfortune of encountering a vomit-flavored bean. Despite his caution, Dumbledore decides to try a golden-brown bean, thinking it to be a safe toffee, only to find it is earwax-flavored.

questions.q: Who visits Harry in the Hospital Wing?
questions.fact: While many fans associate the scene where Dumbledore visits him with the movie, in the book it is actually Ron and Hermione who visit Harry in the hospital wing first to give him candy.

questions.q: What does Hagrid give Harry as an end-of-year gift?
questions.fact: The photo album included pictures of his father and mother, which moved because they were magic. It is a sentimental gift and Hagrid begins to cry.

questions.q: What do the Dursleys not know that Harry cannot do but plans to at home?
options: Send letters to Ron and Hermione
questions.fact: Hagrid reminds him at the end of the book that he is not permitted to perform any magic over the holidays, but the Dursleys don't know that, so Harry cheekily plans to play some pranks.

questions.q: What is the name of the boy at the centre of the Skandar series?
questions.fact: Skandar Smith is named after the author A.F. Steadman's own family — the name has Norse origins. He lives on the Isle of Wight before his life changes forever.

questions.q: In the Skandar series, what are the unicorns like compared to how most people imagine them?
questions.fact: A.F. Steadman deliberately subverted the classic unicorn image. In her world, unicorns are lethal predators that can kill with their horns, teeth and elemental powers — and they bond with riders who share their wild nature.

questions.q: What is the name of the dangerous island where unicorn riders are trained?
questions.fact: The Eyrie is a training school perched on dramatic cliffs. It's a hierarchical, competitive and dangerous world — only the toughest survive.

questions.q: What are the five elements that unicorns in the series are bonded to?
options: Fire, water, earth, air and spirit
Earth, water, fire, air and metal
Fire, water, air, earth and darkness
Light, shadow, fire, storm and earth
questions.fact: Each unicorn is elemental — riders who bond with spirit unicorns are the rarest and most feared. Skandar himself bonds with something even more unusual and dangerous.

questions.q: What is the Chaos Cup in the Skandar series?
options: A deadly race that tests riders and unicorns
A tournament to choose the next Commodore
An annual festival celebrating the island
questions.fact: The Chaos Cup is an incredibly dangerous annual race around the island — riders are frequently killed. Winning it is the highest honour in the unicorn-riding world.

questions.q: What is the name of Skandar's unicorn?
questions.fact: Scoundrel's Luck is a spirit-bonded unicorn — one of the most rare and feared types on the island. The name reflects the chaotic, unpredictable nature of spirit element unicorns.

questions.q: Skandar is a 'Walker' — what does this mean?
options: Someone who arrived at the Eyrie on foot
Someone born on the mainland who crossed to the island
Someone who rides without a saddle
questions.fact: Walkers come from the mainland — they are looked down on by Islanders who were born on the island. Skandar's Walker status is a source of prejudice and difficulty throughout his training.

questions.q: What terrible thing happens to riders whose unicorns are killed?
options: They lose the ability to speak
They are exiled from the island
They are executed by the Commodore
They are left mentally and physically destroyed — called 'Nomads'
questions.fact: Nomads wander the island in a state of madness after losing their unicorn bond. They are one of the most tragic figures in the series — a constant warning to riders of what they stand to lose.

questions.q: Who is the villain in the first book, Skandar and the Unicorn Thief?
questions.fact: The Weaver is a disgraced rider who steals unicorns and their horns to create monstrous new creatures. The mystery of the Weaver's true identity is a central thread across the series.

questions.q: What is the significance of a 'wild unicorn' in the series?
options: A unicorn that has never been ridden
A unicorn bonded to no element
An unbonded unicorn that has escaped the island and terrorises the mainland
A unicorn that can change its elemental power
questions.fact: Wild unicorns that escape to the mainland are catastrophically dangerous, attacking and killing people. Controlling and preventing this is one of the most important jobs of the island's riders and leadership.

questions.q: What are the four training groups at the Eyrie called?
options: Earth, Fire, Water and Air dens
Storm, Flame, Tide and Stone squads
Red, Blue, Gold and Silver stables
questions.fact: Riders are sorted into elemental dens — Earth, Fire, Water and Air — based on their unicorn's element. Spirit-bonded riders like Skandar don't officially fit into any den, which causes him major difficulties.

questions.q: In Skandar and the Phantom Rider, what is the Phantom Rider?
options: A ghost of a dead rider haunting the Eyrie
A legendary unbeatable champion of the Chaos Cup
A masked rider whose identity nobody knows
A rider who has bonded with a ghost unicorn
questions.fact: The Phantom Rider's mysterious identity is central to the second book's mystery. Their extraordinary skill makes them a legend — and Skandar becomes obsessed with uncovering the truth.

questions.q: Which of these is one of Skandar's closest friends at the Eyrie?
questions.fact: Flo Shekoni is one of Skandar's core group of friends. A.F. Steadman created a diverse, tightly-knit quartet — their friendship and loyalty to each other is as important as any action in the series.

questions.q: What is the full title of the first book in the series?
options: Skandar and the Island of Chaos
questions.fact: Skandar and the Unicorn Thief was published in 2022. A.F. Steadman spent years developing the world before it was published — it became an instant bestseller and was praised for reinventing the unicorn in fiction.

questions.q: On which real British island is Skandar Smith living at the start of the story?
questions.fact: Skandar and his father live on the Isle of Wight. His father is struggling after the disappearance of Skandar's mother — her fate and connection to the unicorn world becomes a major mystery across the series.

quizzes.name: West End & Broadway
quizzes.description: Songs, shows and stars of the stage

questions.q: Which musical features the song 'Defying Gravity'?
questions.fact: 'Defying Gravity' closes Act One of Wicked. The musical is based on Gregory Maguire's 1995 novel and tells the untold story of the witches of Oz.

questions.q: In which musical does the character Jean Valjean appear?
questions.fact: Les Misérables is based on Victor Hugo's 1862 novel. The musical opened in London in 1985 and has been running in the West End almost continuously ever since.

questions.q: Which musical is told primarily in hip-hop and rap?
questions.fact: Lin-Manuel Miranda wrote Hamilton after reading Ron Chernow's biography of Alexander Hamilton on a holiday. It opened on Broadway in 2015 and won 11 Tony Awards.

questions.q: Who composed The Phantom of the Opera?
questions.fact: Andrew Lloyd Webber's Phantom opened in London's West End in 1986. It became Broadway's longest-running show, closing after 35 years in April 2023.

questions.q: Which musical features the songs 'Memory' and 'Mr Mistoffelees'?
questions.fact: Cats is based on T.S. Eliot's poetry collection Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats. 'Memory' became one of the best-known songs in musical theatre.

questions.q: In which musical do orphan children sing 'Food, Glorious Food'?
questions.fact: Oliver! is based on Charles Dickens's Oliver Twist. Lionel Bart wrote all the music and lyrics, and it won the Academy Award for Best Picture in its 1968 film adaptation.

questions.q: Which musical is based on songs by ABBA?
questions.fact: Mamma Mia! opened in London's West End in 1999. The story was written around 22 ABBA songs by playwright Catherine Johnson.

questions.q: What does Billy Elliot dream of becoming in the musical of the same name?
questions.fact: Billy Elliot the Musical opened in London in 2005. Elton John wrote the music, and the show won four Olivier Awards in its first year.

questions.q: Which musical tells the story of six wives of Henry VIII?
questions.fact: Six was written by Toby Marlow and Lucy Moss while they were students at Cambridge University. It opened in the West End in 2019 and on Broadway in 2021.

questions.q: Which musical features the song 'Don't Cry For Me Argentina'?
questions.fact: Evita tells the story of Eva Perón, First Lady of Argentina. Andrew Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice wrote the musical, and it opened in London in 1978.

questions.q: In which city is the musical Rent set?
questions.fact: Rent is set in Manhattan's East Village in the early 1990s. Jonathan Larson based it on Puccini's opera La Bohème and tragically died the night before its Off-Broadway premiere.

questions.q: Which musical is set in 1920s Chicago, centring on two women accused of murder?
questions.fact: Chicago is based on real 1920s cases. The musical opened on Broadway in 1975, but its 1996 revival has made it Broadway's longest-running American musical.

questions.q: What is the name of the hero in Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat?
questions.fact: Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat was Andrew Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice's first collaboration. It began as a 15-minute school cantata in 1968.

questions.q: Which character does Elphaba become known as at the end of Wicked?
options: The Good Witch of the North
The Wicked Witch of the West
The Wicked Witch of the East
questions.fact: Elphaba's name is derived from the initials of L. Frank Baum, the author of The Wonderful Wizard of Oz. The musical reimagines the classic story from her perspective.

questions.q: Which famous musical features the characters Marius, Cosette and Éponine?
questions.fact: Victor Hugo created these beloved characters in his 1862 masterpiece. Éponine's unrequited love for Marius gives us one of the show's most emotional songs, 'On My Own'.

quizzes.name: Hamilton
quizzes.description: HamilFans, get your thinking caps on! This one is gonna be hard

questions.q: In Hamilton, which love story never happened?
questions.fact: Lin-Manuel Miranda created a love triangle for drama, but in real life, Angelica was already married when he met her, and they were just friends.

questions.q: How long did it take Lin-Manuel Miranda to write the entire piece?
questions.fact: Lin-Manuel Miranda spent approximately 7 whole years completing this famous 3 hour musical.

questions.q: Who was the original Lafayette in New York?
questions.fact: Daveed Diggs achieved the fastest rap in Broadway history as Marquis de Lafayette in Hamilton, specifically in the song "Guns and Ships". He raps at a rate of 6.3 words per second, or 19 words in just 3 seconds during the fastest, record-breaking portion of the song.

questions.q: In what song did Daveed Diggs achieve fastest rap in Broadway history?
questions.fact: He rapped 6.3 words per second (roughly 378 words per minute). This speed closely matches Eminem’s rate of 6.46 words per second during a 15-second span in "Rap God". The speed is part of the character Lafayette’s development from a student of English to a "rap god/military superhero".  Diggs is also a member of the experimental hip-hop group "clipping" and is recognized for his technical rapping skill outside of theater.

questions.q: When does Hamilton find out about John Lauren’s death?
questions.fact: In the Hamilton musical, Alexander Hamilton learns of John Laurens' death in the scene "Tomorrow There'll Be More Of Us" when Eliza reads a letter from Laurens' father informing them that Laurens was killed in a gunfight on August 27, 1782, in South Carolina. Historically, Hamilton knew by October 12, 1782, when he wrote to General Nathanael Greene.

questions.q: When Hamilton hears about Lauren’s death, what does he almost instantly say?
options: I have so much work to do.
questions.fact: Hamilton staying, in grief, that he has, “so much work to do” transitions perfectly into “Non-Stop”.

questions.q: When Hamilton finds out about Lauren’s death, what song does the scene transition into?
questions.fact: Hamilton immediately launches into the song "Non-Stop".

questions.q: What color dress does Peggy famously wear?
questions.fact: In the musical Hamilton, Peggy Schuyler is famously costumed in a soft yellow dress. Costume designer Paul Tazewell chose this color to highlight her youth, innocence, and her position as the "little sister" compared to her sisters Angelica (pink/orange) and Eliza (blue/green). In the second act, after the character of Peggy has died, the remaining sisters move toward yellow-themed clothing (Eliza in green, Angelica in orange), symbolizing they have "moved toward" her memory.

questions.q: In the “Schuyler Sisters”, which character is highly featured but is not a Schuyler sister or Hamilton?
questions.fact: Immediately following "The Story of Tonight," Burr initiates the song, positioning himself as the observer of the Schuyler sisters', whom he describes as the "envy of all". Burr sets the scene for the 1776 New York City setting, highlighting the energy and danger of the revolution, contrasting his cautious nature with the excitement of the sisters, especially Angelica. Throughout the song, Burr provides commentary, emphasizing his role as the narrator tracking the rise of the Schuyler family and Hamilton's eventual entanglement with them. This appearance reinforces Burr's role as a character who is simultaneously involved in the story and looking back on it to frame the events that lead to his eventual downfall and the duel.

questions.q: What is the name of the lady that Hamilton has an affair with?
questions.fact: The actress who plays Peggy in the first act of the show plays Maria Reynolds in the second as Peggy died at a young age and wasn’t in the later events of the story.

questions.q: What is the famous last thing Eliza does at the end of the show?
options: She plays the piano like a pro
She gets bitten by a crocodile
questions.fact: There is a global debate amongst HamFans what this gasp really means. It could represent her death, it could represent her finding out her husband had died… but the thing is, no one really knows- everyone just has their own opinion.

questions.q: What is the name of the final song that King George 3 sings?
questions.fact: The King sings this in his third and final appearance in Act Two, where he reacts with amusement to the news that George Washington is stepping down and John Adams is becoming president.

questions.q: What song does the King stay onstage for, watching the rest of the cast perform?
questions.fact: The King famously sits on a box through the song, then joins in at the end, mocking Hamilton that he’s, “never gon’ be President now”.

questions.q: Which character changes out of their main costume after The Reynolds Pamphlet?
questions.fact: After this scene, the actor often changes out of the regal King George costume and joins the ensemble on the balcony in a neutral, white costume for the remainder of the show, including the final bow.

questions.q: What are Hamilton fans usually called?
questions.fact: This is the most common term used to describe devotees of the show.

quizzes.name: Movie Magic
quizzes.description: Blockbusters, classics and everything in between

questions.q: Who directed the 1997 film Titanic?
questions.fact: James Cameron spent so much time diving to the actual Titanic wreck for research that he's one of the most experienced deep-sea divers in the world.

questions.q: Which film features the line 'May the Force be with you'?
questions.fact: Star Wars: A New Hope was released in 1977. George Lucas was so convinced it would fail that he went on holiday when it opened — and came back to find massive queues around the block.

questions.q: Who voiced Woody in the Toy Story films?
questions.fact: Tom Hanks has voiced Woody in all four Toy Story films. His brother Jim Hanks voiced Woody in spin-off shorts and video games.

questions.q: Which Disney film features the song 'Let It Go'?
questions.fact: 'Let It Go' won the Academy Award for Best Original Song in 2014. Idina Menzel's recording became one of the best-selling singles of that year.

questions.q: What is the name of the fish Marlin is searching for in Finding Nemo?
questions.fact: Finding Nemo won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature in 2004. Marine biologists have noted that in real life, clownfish actually change sex — so Marlin would have become Nemo's mother!

questions.q: Who played Hermione Granger in the Harry Potter films?
questions.fact: Emma Watson was 9 years old when she auditioned for the role, having no previous professional acting experience. She went on to study at Brown University and Oxford.

questions.q: Which film became the highest-grossing film of all time when released in 2009?
questions.fact: Avatar held the record until Avengers: Endgame briefly overtook it in 2019. Avatar then reclaimed the top spot after its 2022 re-release.

questions.q: Who directed the Lord of the Rings film trilogy?
questions.fact: Peter Jackson filmed all three Lord of the Rings movies simultaneously in New Zealand. The Return of the King won all 11 Academy Awards it was nominated for — a record.

questions.q: What is the name of the young hero in Home Alone?
questions.fact: Home Alone was released in 1990 and became the highest-grossing comedy film of all time at that point. Macaulay Culkin was 10 years old when it was filmed.

questions.q: Which animated film features the character Moana?
questions.fact: Disney's Moana is set in ancient Polynesia. The filmmakers worked closely with Pacific Islander cultural consultants, and the film features original songs by Lin-Manuel Miranda.

questions.q: In which film does a toy cowboy and space ranger become rivals then friends?
questions.fact: Toy Story (1995) was the first feature-length film made entirely with computer-generated imagery. Pixar rendered each frame at a resolution that required up to 90 hours of computing time.

questions.q: Which film features the character Jack Sparrow?
questions.fact: Johnny Depp based his portrayal of Jack Sparrow partly on rock star Keith Richards of the Rolling Stones. Keith Richards later appeared as Jack's father in the third film.

questions.q: Which 2019 South Korean film became the first non-English-language film to win the Oscar for Best Picture?
questions.fact: Parasite, directed by Bong Joon-ho, won four Academy Awards at the 2020 ceremony. Bong famously said in his acceptance speech, 'Once you overcome the one-inch-tall barrier of subtitles, you'll be introduced to so many more amazing films.'

questions.q: Who plays the character Katniss Everdeen in The Hunger Games films?
questions.fact: Jennifer Lawrence was 21 when the first Hunger Games film was released in 2012. She had won the Academy Award for Best Actress just a year later for Silver Linings Playbook.

questions.q: Which Pixar film features a rat who dreams of becoming a chef in Paris?
questions.fact: Ratatouille won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature in 2008. The film's title is both the name of the main character (Rémy) and a traditional French vegetable dish.

quizzes.name: Star Wars Saga
quizzes.description: Episodes I–IX — the nine core saga films only

questions.q: In which Star Wars film does Anakin Skywalker win his freedom by competing in a podrace?
questions.fact: The Boonta Eve Classic podrace on Tatooine in The Phantom Menace (1999) is one of the longest action sequences in the saga. Anakin is the only human fast enough to race.

questions.q: What is Order 66?
options: A command to arrest all Jedi
A command to execute all Sith
A command to destroy all clones
A command to surrender to the Empire
questions.fact: Order 66 is executed in Revenge of the Sith (2005). It was a pre-programmed directive embedded in every clone trooper's inhibitor chip, compelling them to turn on their Jedi generals.

questions.q: Which planet is destroyed by the Death Star in A New Hope?
questions.fact: Alderaan is destroyed on the orders of Grand Moff Tarkin to demonstrate the Death Star's power. Princess Leia is forced to watch. Alderaan had no weapons and had been a peaceful planet.

questions.q: On which frozen planet does the Rebel Alliance have their base at the start of The Empire Strikes Back?
questions.fact: Echo Base on Hoth is discovered by an Imperial probe droid, triggering the Battle of Hoth — one of the most iconic opening sequences in the saga.

questions.q: What does Darth Vader reveal to Luke Skywalker in The Empire Strikes Back?
options: That the Emperor is his grandfather
That Luke has a twin sister
That Obi-Wan was once a Sith
questions.fact: The reveal 'No, I am your father' is widely considered the greatest plot twist in cinema history. Very few people on set knew — even Mark Hamill was only told the night before filming.

questions.q: What species are the Ewoks who help the Rebels in Return of the Jedi?
options: Small teddy-bear-like creatures living on the forest moon of Endor
Reptilian warriors from the swamps of Dagobah
Amphibious creatures from the oceans of Mon Calamari
Small green creatures from the caves of Tatooine
questions.fact: George Lucas originally planned for the battle on Endor to feature Wookiees, but changed them to Ewoks to show that primitive creatures could defeat a technologically advanced empire.

questions.q: Who trained Obi-Wan Kenobi as a Padawan learner?
questions.fact: Qui-Gon Jinn was a maverick Jedi who often clashed with the Jedi Council. He was killed by Darth Maul at the end of The Phantom Menace, making Obi-Wan promise to train Anakin.

questions.q: What is the name of Han Solo's ship?
questions.fact: Han Solo famously boasts that the Millennium Falcon 'made the Kessel Run in less than twelve parsecs'. Han won the ship from Lando Calrissian in a card game of sabacc.

questions.q: In The Force Awakens, what is the name of the new superweapon used by the First Order?
questions.fact: Starkiller Base is built into a planet rather than a space station — making it far larger than either Death Star. It draws power from a star, eventually consuming it entirely.

questions.q: Who is Rey revealed to be the granddaughter of in The Rise of Skywalker?
questions.fact: The Rise of Skywalker reveals that Rey is Emperor Palpatine's granddaughter. Her father was Palpatine's son who rejected the dark side and went into hiding to protect her.

questions.q: What colour is Mace Windu's lightsaber — unique among the Jedi in the films?
questions.fact: Samuel L. Jackson requested a purple lightsaber so he could spot himself in the large battle scenes. George Lucas initially refused but eventually agreed.

questions.q: On which volcanic planet does the climactic duel between Obi-Wan and Anakin take place in Revenge of the Sith?
questions.fact: Mustafar is an industrial mining planet covered in rivers of lava. The duel leaves Anakin horrifically burned, requiring the life support suit that transforms him into Darth Vader.

questions.q: What are the last words Yoda says before he dies in Return of the Jedi?
options: 'When gone am I, the last of the Jedi will you be'
questions.fact: 'There is another Skywalker' hints at the twin sibling reveal. It echoes a line Yoda says to Obi-Wan's ghost in The Empire Strikes Back.

questions.q: Which character says 'I've got a bad feeling about this' in The Phantom Menace?
questions.fact: 'I've got a bad feeling about this' appears in every single Star Wars saga film. When Poe Dameron starts to say it in The Last Jedi, BB-8 cuts him off.

questions.q: In Attack of the Clones, on which ocean planet does Obi-Wan discover the secret clone army?
questions.fact: Kamino is a planet of perpetual storms and endless ocean, home to the galaxy's finest cloners. It had been erased from the Jedi Archive — deliberately deleted by Count Dooku years earlier.
